text.tspan: COMPUTE RESOURCE CONSOLIDATION: pack many small workloads into a
single compute unit (container, VM, function) to amortize cost &
overhead. The opposite of 'one tiny service per VM'.
BAD: ONE TINY THING PER VM
10 microservices u00d7 dedicated VM each u00d7 idle 90% of time
= paying for 10 mostly-idle VMs
= OS overhead per VM (RAM, patch surface, monitoring agents)
= poor utilization of CPU/RAM
= orchestration complexity for tiny things
WHEN TO CONSOLIDATE
u2022 services have similar SLAs (scaling them together is fine)
u2022 services have COMPLEMENTARY load patterns (peaks at different times)
u2022 services share trust boundary (don't co-locate untrusted code)
u2022 operational overhead per service is high
u2022 cost-sensitive workloads (small companies, dev environments)
WHEN NOT TO CONSOLIDATE
u2022 services have very different scaling needs
u2022 different security/compliance domains
u2022 noisy-neighbor risk (one service can starve others)
u2022 different language/runtime constraints
u2022 fault isolation matters more than density
PITFALLS
u2022 NOISY NEIGHBOR: one service spikes; co-tenants suffer
u2022 FAULT BLAST RADIUS: container/VM crash takes down N services
u2022 DEPLOY COUPLING: harder to deploy services independently
u2022 RESOURCE STARVATION: without good limits, one hog wins
u2022 OBSERVABILITY: harder to attribute resource usage per service
BIN PACKING (orchestrator)
Kubernetes / Nomad packs many pods onto fewer nodes
workloads stay in separate containers (isolation)
but nodes are well-utilized
resource requests/limits drive packing
MULTI-WORKLOAD CONTAINER
small daemons/cron jobs combined into one container
with supervisord / s6 / monit
good for utility processes; weakens single-responsibility
SHARED PROCESS / IN-PROC PLUGINS
several lightweight services as plugins/modules in one process
shared OS, shared runtime, shared memory
highest density; weakest isolation
e.g. Envoy filters, Nginx modules
SERVERLESS (Lambda/Cloud Run/Functions)
PLATFORM does the consolidation transparently
many functions share the underlying fleet
cost only for invocations
